query: ok i think the app is basically done — can you go through my repo and tell me what i forgot before i put it in front of paying customers? it's a next.js + supabase thing
we're launching the API next Tuesday. can you review it and flag anything that could blow up in prod? especially the stripe webhook handler
starting a fresh SaaS today (rails + postgres + an openai feature). set me up properly from the start so i don't regret it in 6 months — CI, tests, the works
my background sync job sometimes duplicates rows and once wiped a user's data when it crashed halfway. can you look at how it writes to the db and tell me what's structurally wrong?
is this thing ready to ship? it's a node service that talks to the shopify api and runs a nightly cron
audit my codebase for the stuff teams usually miss — error handling on external calls, secret management, idempotency, that kind of thing
before i merge this PR that adds the email-sending feature, what should i be checking? we really don't want to accidentally spam people or double-send
i keep getting surprise openai bills. can you go through everywhere we call the api and tell me what guardrails we're missing?
help me harden my web app before launch next month — what tests am i missing, do i need CI, what about backups and rate limiting?
my production database is down RIGHT NOW and users are getting 500s — help me figure out why and get it back up asap
write me a github actions workflow that deploys to vercel on every push to main
explain what a two-way door decision is, i saw the term in a blog post about leadership
review this pull request for code style and naming consistency — are my variable names clear and is the formatting idiomatic?
what's the cleanest way to unit test a pure function that calculates tax brackets? jest or vitest?
my rate limiter is rejecting legit users, here's the redis config — can you spot why it's miscounting?
set up prettier and eslint in my repo with the standard config
can you help me write the marketing landing page copy for our product launch?
is postgres or mysql a better choice for a multi-tenant b2b saas? just want your opinion
